author.name: Abdulla Qahhor
author.description: Abdulla Qahhor (1907-1978) was a prominent Uzbek writer, dramatist, and journalist who played a significant role in Uzbek literature.

works.title: O'G'RIM
works.description: One of the most significant works of Abdulla Qahhor, depicting the life and struggles of common people.

works.title: Sinchalak
works.description: A famous novella that tells about the life of a young girl.

works.title: BEMOR
works.description: A historical drama exploring themes of illness and recovery.

works.title: Paxta Dehqonlari
works.description: A novel about cotton farmers and their lives.

works.title: Shayton Uyi
works.description: A dramatic work exploring moral and societal issues.

quotes.text: Adabiyot - bu millatning ruhiy durdonasi va qalbi tutni ifodasidir.
quotes.translation: Literature is the spiritual treasure of a nation and the expression of its heart's desire.

quotes.text: To'liq insonlik - bu faqat madaniyat va ilm odam beradi.
quotes.translation: True humanity is given to a person only by culture and knowledge.

quotes.text: Yozmaloq yasashni to'g'ri qiymatlantirishga olib keladi.
quotes.translation: Writing leads to correct appreciation of life.

quotes.text: Bosh asarda inson sifati, uning o'n hayoti va ishlari yuzasida fikr yuritiladi.
quotes.translation: The main theme of the work is about human nature, his daily life and deeds.

biography.earlyLife: Abdulla Qahhor was born in Tashkent in 1907. He grew up during a time of significant change in Central Asia.
biography.education: He received his education during the early Soviet period and was influenced by both traditional Uzbek culture and modern progressive ideas.
biography.careerStart: He began his literary career in the late 1920s, initially publishing stories and poetry.
biography.majorWorks: Throughout the 1930s and 1940s, he wrote several major works that became classics of Uzbek literature.
biography.laterLife: In his later years, he focused on drama and journalism, continuing to contribute to Uzbek culture until his death in 1978.
biography.legacy: Abdulla Qahhor is considered one of the most important figures in Uzbek literature, known for his realistic portrayal of ordinary people's lives and his exploration of social themes.

timeline.event: Passed away, leaving a significant legacy

facts: He was one of the founders of modern Uzbek literature
His works were translated into many languages
He was also known for his journalism and criticism
He played an important role in the development of Uzbek drama
His works often dealt with social issues and ordinary people's lives
He was respected by both Soviet and post-Soviet literary communities
